message:
fix(combinator): use saturating arithmetic and strengthen all_succeeded invariant

bulkhead.rs:
- Replace raw u128→u64 cast with u64::try_from(...).unwrap_or(u64::MAX)
  for queue_timeout.as_millis() to handle Duration::MAX without truncation.
- Use saturating_add for deadline_millis computation to prevent overflow
  when now_millis is near u64::MAX and the timeout is very large.
- Add test enqueue_deadline_saturates_for_large_timeout verifying that
  Duration::MAX timeout at time u64::MAX yields deadline u64::MAX
  instead of wrapping around.

retry.rs:
- Replace total += Duration::from_nanos(...) with total.saturating_add(...)
  in total_delay_budget() to prevent Duration overflow panic when policy
  specifies very large initial delays with a multiplier.
- Replace self.total_delay += delay with saturating_add in
  RetryState::next_attempt() for the same reason during live retries.
- Add test retry_state_total_delay_saturates verifying that accumulating
  into Duration::MAX doesn't panic but clamps at Duration::MAX.
- Add test total_delay_budget_accumulates_large_delays verifying the
  budget function also saturates correctly with u64::MAX-nanos delays.

map_reduce.rs:
- Add && self.reduced.is_some() guard to all_succeeded() so a
  malformed MapReduceResult with successes but no reduced value
  (e.g. from manual construction) doesn't falsely report success.
  This prevents callers from unwrapping reduced after checking
  all_succeeded() on a structurally inconsistent result.
- Add test map_reduce_result_missing_reduction_not_all_succeeded
  verifying this defensive invariant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
